candidates.stmt: I am Gunjan Patel, standing as your council candidate for Alperton, Wembley, with a clear commitment to our community. I believe in practical solutions that make a real difference—cleaner streets, safer neighbourhoods, and stronger support for local families and businesses. My goal is to bring people together, listen carefully, and act on what matters most to residents. Looking ahead, I want to help create a vibrant, sustainable Alperton where everyone has the opportunity to succeed. With your support, I will work hard to represent you and deliver positive change for our area. Mobile : 07448420987 .Day or Night .I am always available for our Local community.Thank you ✨️

With trepidation, humility, special thanks to Almighty God and to my colleagues at Brent North Conservative Party, I Mabel Balogun look forward to standing as a Conservative councillor in the local election on Thursday 5th May 2022, alongside my nominee, Harmit Vyas.   It is a great privilege to be standing in a ward, I love and which has been home for the last three decades. As a councillor, I pledge to be accountable, available, efficient and effective. I will work hard to ensure zero tolerance to potholes, broken pavements, fly tipping, crime & anti-social behaviors.  As a chartered accountant, I will scrutinize costs,  advocate for good stewardship of  resources and preserve our greenspaces.

Kathleen Fraser was brought up in Wembley. Former College teacher and Carer Kathleen now works as a mental health ‘expert by experience’ and sees mental ill-health as the biggest challenge to the future of Brent residents. Kathleen supported residents in the design of Chalkhill Park and has organised activities, events, food support plus summer holiday schemes for children since 2007. Building the skills, resilience and confidence of all young people is a major priority. Kathleen currently organises a weekly food-waste club and ‘Drop-in’ at Chalkhill Community Centre. She will endeavour to end social isolation with the provision of safe spaces for residents to meet, connect, share skills and ideas for a healthier, cleaner, safer Brent.

My name is William Relton. I am honoured and delighted to have been selected as the candidate for the Green Party in Brent Central. This really is the most important election for decades. The timing is not right for a proper General Election on the broader issues concerning most people up and down the country, which is how a General Election should work. This election will be forever remembered as the Brexit election. Just as Caroline Lucas has been campaigning ever since the ’16 referendum, we really should have had a proper People’s Choice referendum before this election. However, Parliament, or at least the two largest parties, has decided that we should have an election now, so here we are.

This will be a very important election for the smaller parties. People have really begun to realise that our two party, first past the post system, just doesn’t work anymore. A total reform of our electoral system is urgently needed. A vote for the smaller parties is a step in the right direction towards Proportional Representation, a system that the vast majority of modern democracies use. The Lib Dems are still badly tainted by their record in the coalition government with the Tories. A vote for the Green Party will help support a movement for a fairer, cleaner, happier Britain. Please do vote for me, William Relton, Green Party, Brent Central on December 12.

Bastôn De’Medici-Jaguar was born and raised in Jamaica. He migrated to the UK seven years ago, where he read law at university. After completing his training as a barrister, Bastôn was called to the Degree of the Utter Bar in 2023 by the Honourable Society of the Middle Temple. 

Before migrating to the UK, Bastôn worked for four years in the service industry and another four in education. Whilst reading for his law degree, he worked with asylum-seekers (who had exhausted all rights of appeal), assisting them in submitting fresh claims to the Home Office. Bastôn also worked as a policy researcher with the Environmental Law Foundation, investigating the state of affairs of Local Wildlife Sites and making policy recommendations to local authorities on how to improve these sites and cultivate a more cohesive ecosystem.  

Driven by social injustice and a desire to be more involved in the community, Bastôn joined the Green Party and was selected by his local party to stand in the 2022 local elections in Kenton.  As a Green Party member and if elected to parliament as an MP for Brent West, Bastôn will champion: •	a call for a poverty emergency  •	a ceasefire and recognition of a sovereign Palestine •	overhauling the disability welfare system to treat disabled people with dignity •	affordable housing & a charter to protect renters and •	food security

Bastôn believes there is presently not much difference between the two largest parties, which have moved from the original premise on which they were founded, resulting in a stagnation of long-term policies that affect society at large. As such, Bastôn strongly believes in the need to engage in honest conversations on the difficult decisions that need to be made to ensure we have a prosperous future for generations to come.  

A vote for the Greens is NOT a wasted vote, but a vote for real hope and real change!

I’m standing as your Green Party candidate for Harlesden and Kensal Green because our community deserves safer streets, better housing and a cleaner, healthier environment.
Too many residents are dealing with unsafe roads, poor pavement conditions and housing that is not meeting people’s needs. I want to focus on practical improvements that make everyday life better, from safer junctions and more walkable streets to fairer, more secure housing.
As part of a strong local Green team, I will work hard to stand up for residents, improve public spaces and ensure our neighbourhood is a place everyone can feel proud of.

Let me introduce myself.  My name is Ian Collier and I am your Reform Party parliamentary candidate for the newly formed constituency of Brent West.

I have lived in Brent for all of my 73 years.  I went to school in Willesden and Kilburn and I got married in Kingsbury.  I am a qualified actuary and graduated in mathematics and economics from the University of Sussex in 1972.

I have three children who all went to school in Brent and have eight grandchildren.

I have been a member of the Woodcock Park Bowling Club for 35 years and served as its president for two years.

I am a keen football and cricket lover, supporting Chelsea FC and Middlesex CCC and have interests in a Child Care business and a Domiciliary Care business.

I joined Reform UK over eighteen months ago after having believed in conservative values all my life and distraught that the so-called Conservative Party had abandoned these principles which are the basis of Reform’s philosophy.  I discovered that Reform was made up of ordinary people like you and me who have a much better handle on life than the career politicians that swamp the other parties.

A Message to the People of Kilburn
Kilburn deserves better — safer streets, stronger community, and a council that listens.
I’m standing because too many people feel ignored. You see it in rising crime, struggling high streets, and services that don’t reflect the taxes you pay. That isn’t good enough.
My focus is simple:
Safer streets with visible, accountable policing
Support for local businesses and our pubs — the heart of Kilburn
Responsible spending at Brent Council — your money, properly managed
Kilburn is a place of character, faith, and community. From our churches to our local pubs, it’s where people come together. We should protect that — not watch it decline.
I’m not a career politician. I’m here to represent you — to listen, to act, and to bring common sense back into local decisions.
If you want change, it starts here.
Vote Lester Blent — Reform UK, Kilburn

As a long-time Brent resident, I decided to run for council because I can no longer stand by while my community and public spaces deteriorate and crime becomes normalised on our streets.

I am standing to represent hardworking people by fighting for safer streets, lower taxes, and the protection of personal freedoms. As an AI consultant, I understand the challenges that working people will face in the years ahead, and I am committed to ensuring our borough is prepared for that future.

I will work to make housing more affordable for young people, push for the firm and consistent prosecution of anti-social behaviour, and eliminate wasteful spending and cronyism from the incumbent Labour council.

I am a local resident and business owner. I want us and especially our children to feel safer on our streets as I have seen a surge in shoplifting and knife crime in the past few years. I also would like to see more good shops open on Kilburn High Road and a better outlook for businesses.
Holding Brent's Labour council to account.

-Sort that pigeon problem under Windermere bridge!!! Too much guano causing e. Coli amongst residents! 
-get rid of gang warfare in Preston road
-reduce flytipping and ASBs from HMOs/illegal HMOs
-introduce French drains in the residents gardens (or unblock them) to remove water logging. 
- want even paving so our vulnerable can walk/run outside
-Introduce recycling of glass bottles and cans with money returned like they do in Europe 
- want to get rid of the blue bag which is a nuisance (find another way) 
-make sure our recycling is actually being recycled and not put on a rubbish heap
-make sure all roads are gritted during the winter
-I am an anti racist and anti prejudice, unless you commit anti social behaviour I don't have a problem with all walks of life.

I've lived in Queen's Park for 23 years. I've brought up a daughter here, been a governor of local schools, a Queen’s Park councillor for two years and I'm passionate about our area.

Queen's Park is a great place to live. But too many families find it hard to make ends meet. I’m glad Brent’s Resident Support Fund (RSF) can help give families a lifeline when they need it.  We need affordable housing, especially for young families and I’m proud that Brent’s Labour Council is building affordable homes. Brent's private landlord licensing scheme is making a real difference to tenants’ security and living standards.

We have waited far too long for Queens Park station to be made accessible. Parents are still lugging pushchairs up stairs and people with disabilities and older residents have no step free access. We’ve been pressing DfT for step free access for years and we need to keep the pressure up.  We also need to work with local residents to make sure that we manage traffic to keep children safe and reduce congestion where we can.

We need a cleaner, greener, more affordable borough. 
- We’re doubling Brent water enforcement squad and hosting more community skips to keep our streets clean. We’re cracking down on fly tips and prosecuting those who blight our neighbourhoods.
- We’ll commit at least £5 million every year to upgrade our roads and pavements.
- We’re adding solar panels to schools and public buildings to improve sustainability and reduce costs. And we’re rolling out more trees to green our streets and improve air quality. 
- Our education maintenance allowance and school uniform grants will help relieve pressure on families. 
- Our new community safety hubs will mean you’re never more than a 20 minute walk from your local policing team.

From Kensal Green to Kensal Rise to Queen’s Park we’re making your local voices heard.

Vote for Neil, Marc and Lesley - the team you can trust - on 7th May. For a cleaner, safer, more affordable Brent.

Simon Cheng has a strong record of grassroots campaigning and frontline service in Brent. As a housing support worker, he supports residents facing homelessness, overcrowding and housing disrepair, giving him a clear understanding of the pressures many local families are under and what practical help can make the biggest difference.

Originally from Hong Kong, Simon came to the UK as a refugee after being targeted for his pro-democracy activism. Britain gave him safety and the chance to rebuild his life, and he is determined to give back by standing up for fairness, dignity and opportunity for all.

Simon founded a community organisation supporting Hongkongers and other new arrivals under the BN(O) route. Through this work he has helped thousands of migrants and refugees to settle, access services and thrive, strengthening community cohesion and building bridges between neighbours. His leadership has been recognised nationally, reflecting the values of solidarity, equality and public service that he brings to everything he does.

Since joining Labour, Simon has served as a ward organiser, helping to lead campaigns across Brent and working closely with local members and residents. Known for his energy, teamwork and reliability, he is committed to building an inclusive, people-powered Labour movement that listens and delivers.

Simon believes Labour is the only party with the values and the plan to bring real change to Queensbury. He will fight for decent, secure housing, strong local services and climate action that improves daily life, and he will always be a visible councillor who works hard and follows through. With lived experience as a refugee, daily work supporting local people, and a clear commitment to human rights, Simon will stand up for every resident and help Queensbury move forward together.

- CRACK DOWN ON CRIME:  In the last 6 months, Roundwood Ward had 107 Drug related offences and over 300+ cases of Violence. We will lobby for more police patrol and adult/youth support 
in our war against criminal activity.
SUPPORT LOCAL BUSINESS: Introduce 30 minute free parking, push for Business Rates Relief for local businesses.
FLY-TIPPING AND POTHOLES: Fight against the rampant fly tipping, by introducing more bins and 3 free Bulky waste collections per year. Invest further in quality pothole repairs that last!

As an Independent candidate for Stonebridge, my priority is simple: putting our neighbourhood before party politics.

For too long, our ward has been treated as a safe seat for established parties who often prioritise town hall agenda's over the lived experiences of people on the St Raphael’s and Stonebridge estates. I am standing to ensure that when decisions are made about our homes, our community centres, and our future, your voice is the loudest one in the room.

I believe in local representation that is visible, accessible, and accountable. Working alongside fellow independent James Rossi, our "Vote for Your Neighbour" campaign is about reclaiming our community’s power. We don’t answer to party whips or distant leaders; we answer only to you.

My Pledges to Stonebridge Residents:

Estate-First Representation: I will be a dedicated advocate for the residents of the entire ward, ensuring our estates receive the investment and maintenance they deserve.

Protecting Community Assets: I am committed to the future of the Bridge Park Complex and will fight to ensure it remains a hub that serves the local people who built its legacy.

Direct Accountability: As an Independent, I have no party line to follow. My votes in the Council will always be based on what is best for Stonebridge, not what is convenient for a political party.

Transparency & Presence: I won't just appear at election time. I live here, I work here, and I will maintain a regular, open presence to tackle the "on-the-ground" issues—from fly-tipping to housing repairs—that often get ignored.

On 7 May 2026, you have a choice: more of the same, or a neighbour who will truly stand up for you.
Independent

I am standing as an Independent candidate because our community deserves representation that puts residents and constituents first — not party politics.

Too many people feel unheard while dealing with real issues like safety, rising costs, rubbish, drug-related crime, and anti-social behaviour. I also believe the current leadership is getting it wrong — our area cannot be managed by a party that sets guidelines you must follow, rather than responding to what local residents and constituents actually need.

As an Independent, I will be accountable only to you. My focus is simple:

- Cleaner streets
- Safer neighbourhoods
- Tackling drug-related crime and anti-social behaviour
- Real investment in Stonebridge
- Listening to residents and constituents — and acting on what you say

I believe we can do better, and I’m committed to working hard to make that happen.

Let’s put Stonebridge first.

Paul Lorber has lived in Brent since 1969—first in Willesden and then in Sudbury and Wembley.

He was educated at Kilburn Comprehensive School and then Manchester University.

He was first elected to Brent Council in 1982 and served for 32 consecutive years until 2014. He was re-elected as a Liberal Democrat Councillor for Sudbury Ward in May 2022.

He has served as a School Governor since 1994 and he helps to run a Community Library in Barham Park as a full-time volunteer.

Paul is passionate about local environmental issues and campaigns on them all year round.

My name is Neha Patel, and I am proud to stand as a council candidate for Sudbury.

I have lived in this area for more than 10 years, and Sudbury is truly my home. My family is rooted here, with many of my relatives living locally, and my son attending school in the community. Like many residents, my daily life is closely connected to the area, and I understand the needs and concerns of local families.

Over the years, I have seen how important it is to have a strong, caring, and active voice in the community. I am passionate about making Sudbury a better place for everyone, where families feel safe, supported, and heard.

I am committed to listening to residents, understanding their concerns, and working hard to represent them honestly and effectively. My focus is on improving our neighbourhood, supporting local services, and helping build a positive future for the next generation.

I believe that together, we can create a stronger and more connected Sudbury.

My focus is simple: protect services, support local businesses, and keep Sudbury a great place to live.

I would be honoured to have your support.

I believe in small government, traditional values, honesty and freedom. Politicians are here to look after the citizens, not rule over them. I am against all road restrictions such as humps and chicanes and all 20mph zones. They make journeys harder for all vehicles including buses and emergency services. I would like to see free parking in shopping centres which would encourage more people to use local shops and could raise more tax in increased sales. I believe no one should ever be treated differently based on their identity, whether for offering employment or educational positions or any other form of discrimination for or against someone.

I am standing for Wembley Central Councillor candidate. I will fight to reduce crime and tackle anti social behaviours. I will make sure council tax are used sensibly.Safe and clean roads. Support local business and working people.Support in Health care system in the community.  Irene Lobo

After living in Wembley Hill for 63 years, over the last ten to twelve years, i believe the council let us down on so many things, our bin collections are a ridiculous twice a month, they gave us not fit for purpose blue recycling bags for cardboard, made the dump harder and harder to use, made rubbish disposal more and more expensive, leading to fly tipping being the norm not the exception.
Our children and grand children not safe on the streets, our schools the same, parking at a ridiculous cost to just park near your home, car less flats that due to a loophole this just means cars cant park there but fill our CPZ, residents asking for basic human needs ignored and police stations front desk closing at 10, yet the council tax rises to the 4.99 limit, that is why residents asked me to run and  at least try to improve the council and our circumstances, that's why I'm running on May 7 th to improve mine and your lives, and I'm one of your own, born and bred in Wembley Hill.
Promoted by andi porri on behalf of andi porri at 7 linden avenue, wembley,middlesex.ha98bb.
Your issues are my issues.

I’m proud to live and work in Wembley Park and to call this vibrant, diverse community my home. I was born in Hong Kong, and spent 15 years working as an educator before moving to London after living in China, Thailand and Vietnam. Today, I live in the ward and work at Wembley Stadium and The Hive – and I often joke that I spend more than 95% of my time right here.

As a Liberal Democrat candidate, I care deeply about tackling anti-social behaviour, improving safety, standing up for renters and leaseholders, and ensuring public transport is working efficiently. I’m also passionate about supporting newcomers and making sure residents and Wembley Stadium can live side by side in harmony.

If elected, I will be a visible, hard-working local councillor, listening to residents, standing up for fairness, and working with different stakeholders to get things done for Wembley Park.

Local resident, business owner, community champion,
and chair of our local business association. I’m
fed up with ongoing Wembley Park roadworks, and I
know many of you are too. If elected, fixing this
disruption will be my first priority. I believe we
need a change in leadership locally and am
committed to practical solutions, stronger
representation, and improving our community
Working towards social and environmental justice.
